title: Health care providers: Report an adverse reaction to a drug - Canada.ca
content:
## Report an adverse reaction to a drug: health care provider
Health care providers who suspect an adverse reaction (side effect) to a drug are strongly encouraged to report it. Talk to your patients about signs to watch for.
If  you are a health care professional who works in a hospital, there are new  mandatory reporting rules and a different  form that you will need to use to report. Check with  your administration to learn about any new requirements for your specific  hospital.
Report an adverse reaction
To mail your report to a Canada Vigilance Regional Office , use the postage paid label .

## What drugs are included
- pharmaceutical drugs (prescription and non-prescription drugs)
- biologics (biotechnology products and blood products)
- radiopharmaceutical drugs
- Adverse reaction database
- Canada Vigilance Program
- Drug and health product register
- Advisories, warnings and recalls
